Defendant We Work Management Llc And Wework Companies, Inc. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of All Proceedings Memorandum Of Supporting Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jan-27-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WE WORK MANAGEMENT LLC, WEWORK COMPANIES INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of All Proceedings.

Defendant We Work Management LLC and WeWork Companies Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. The arbitration agreement includes a clear and unmistakable delegation clause. (Kelly Decl., Ex. 1 [WeWork Employee Dispute Resolution Program, 3 ["The Arbitrator, and not a court, shall have exclusive authority to resolve any dispute relating to the interpretation, applicability, enforceability or formation of this Agreement, including without limitation disputes regarding the procedural or substantive arbitrability of any claim,; and objections with respect to the existence, scope or validity of the Agreement, and any claim that that all or any part of this Agreement is void or voidable."].) Such a clause is enforceable. (E.g., Aanderud v. Superior Court (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 880, 892; Malone v. Superior Court (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 1551, 1560.)

The agreement also states that the JAMS Employment Arbitration Rules & Procedures will apply, and JAMS Rule 11(b) clearly and unmistakably provides that the arbitrator determines "the formation, existence, validity, interpretation or scope of the agreement under which Arbitration is sought." (Kelly Decl., Ex. 1 2; see Dream Theater, Inc. v. Dream Theater (2004) 124 Cal.App.4th 547, 557 ["The Contract provides that if a contested claim is not settled within the contractual deadline, then it must be submitted to binding arbitration in accordance with the AAA Commercial Arbitration Rules. These rules specify that the arbitrator will decide disputes over the scope of the arbitration agreement. We conclude that the parties' agreement to arbitrate according to this rule is clear and unmistakable evidence of the intent that the arbitrator will decide whether a Contested Claim is arbitrable."]; Rodriguez v. American Technologies, Inc. (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 1110, 1123 [parties designated arbitrator to determine scope of arbitration by referring to AAA Construction Industry Rules].)

The court rejects plaintiff's argument that defendants waived the right to arbitrate because they did not mediate and further rejects plaintiff's unconscionability argument. Plaintiff does not argue that the delegation clause itself, which is severable, is unconscionable. Therefore, the arbitrator is charged with determining whether the agreement to arbitrate the underlying merits is viable. (See Rent-A-Center, West, Inc. v. Jackson (2010) 561 U.S. 63, 72 [party must specifically challenge delegation clause; plaintiff's general unconscionability arguments were insufficient to render the delegation clause inapplicable]; Nielsen Contracting, Inc. v. Applied Underwriter, Inc. (2018) 22 Cal.App.5th 1096, 1108 ["[A]n argument that the arbitration agreement or the underlying contract is unenforceable is not sufficient to trigger the court's obligation to resolve contentions regarding the enforceability of a severable delegation clause."]; Aanderud v. Superior Court, 13 Cal.App.5th at 891 [party failed to show that delegation clause was substantively unconscionable].)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-22-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael Bracamontes, Esq. of Bracamontes & Vlasak, T:415.835.6777, for plaintiff/moving party;
Spencer Davidson, Esq. of Jackson Lewis PC, T:415.796.5451, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. After argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF RICHARD MARKEL's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO file first amended complaint is granted. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. (See, e.g., Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428 ["The policy favoring amendment is so strong that it is a rare case in which denial of leave to amend can be justified."].) If Defendant wishes to challenge the validity of the amended claims or of Plaintiff's naming an additional party, they may do so by way of demurrer or motion for summary judgment. (See Brown v. Ralphs Grocery Co. (2018) 28 Cal.App.5th 824, 843 ["The 'better course of action' is to allow a plaintiff to amend the complaint' and then let the parties test its legal sufficiency in other appropriate proceedings.'"].) Defendant has not shown it would be prejudiced by allowing the amendment.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
